codeReview.summary: The LLM integration in CoderAgent.execute() is correctly implemented. The code properly checks for LLM availability (self.llm.is_some()), builds a well-structured prompt requesting JSON with new_content field, handles both successful LLM responses with valid JSON and various failure modes (invalid JSON parsing, LLM errors), and falls back to heuristic approach when LLM is unavailable. All 5 CoderAgent tests pass including test_coder_agent_with_llm_and_tools which verifies the LLM integration path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker modified multiple files beyond the assigned feature scope. The feature was specifically 'fix-coder-agent-llm' targeting CoderAgent, but the commit shows changes to execution.rs, scheduler.rs (orchestrator), sqlite.rs (state), and changes in other crates (swell-llm, swell-tools, swell-state). The AGENTS.md documents the feature-based workflow but doesn't explicitly require atomic commits per feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 7b83c56 --stat shows: crates/swell-orchestrator/src/agents.rs, execution.rs, scheduler.rs, crates/swell-state/src/sqlite.rs, and others modified. Feature ID is fix-coder-agent-llm but changes span multiple subsystems.

summary: This is a re-review of the fix-coder-agent-llm feature. The prior review (7b83c56) already passed this feature. The code review confirms the LLM backend integration in CoderAgent.execute() is correctly implemented: when self.llm is Some, it now calls llm.chat() with a properly formatted prompt requesting JSON with new_content field, parses the response, and uses the returned content. When LLM is unavailable or fails, it correctly falls back to the heuristic approach. All 5 CoderAgent tests pass. The prior sharedStateObservation about multi-file commit scope remains as a non-blocking convention observation - the core implementation is correct and meets the feature requirements.
